title: nubia NaviX Ultra key specifications tipped
description: Last week, nubia unveiled the NaviX Ultra in China, but only revealed its design and color options and kept its hardware details under wraps. However, a tipster has now leaked the smartphone's key specifications. In a now-deleted post, tipster Bald Panda claimed that the nubia NaviX Ultra will be powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset. According to the tipster, the handset will feature a 6.78-inch display with a 1.5K resolution and a refresh rate of 144Hz. The NaviX Ultra is also tipped to offer a triple 50MP rear camera setup, a 50MP front-facing camera, and a...

title: vivo S2 India launch could take place on August 6, design and key specs leak
description: A recent leak claimed that vivo is preparing to launch the S2 to India as the successor to the vivo S1, which debuted in 2019. New leaks have now revealed the phone's launch date, first sale date, design, and key specs. Tipster Abhishek Yadav has claimed on X that the vivo S2 will launch in India on August 6, with sales expected to begin on August 11. The handset is tipped to be available in 8GB/128GB and 8GB/256GB configurations and in Regal Bronze, Sapphire Blue, and Silk White shades. Meanwhile, another leak has revealed the phone's design, which appears similar to the Vivo...

title: OnePlus N6x's launch date announced, design revealed
description: OnePlus introduced the N6 as the first smartphone in its new N Series last month, and over a week ago, it started teasing the N6x as the next member of the series. Today, the Chinese brand announced that the N6x will launch on July 31. Arriving earlier than x-pected. Get notified: https://t.co/M8qRbJlsGF pic.twitter.com/63Q14SmSO6— OnePlus India (@OnePlus_IN) July 23, 2026 Additionally, OnePlus gave us our best look yet at the N6x, which will come in two colors - Burgundy Red and Ice Blue. OnePlus N6x's color options The OnePlus N6x has a flat design, with its back panel...

title: New leaked renders reveal every color option for the Pixel 11 lineup
description: We're just weeks away from Google's Pixel 11 series launch, and by now, almost everything about the upcoming lineup has leaked. A fresh leak has now revealed the complete color lineup for the Pixel 11, Pixel 11 Pro, and Pixel 11 Pro XL. The latest renders, shared by MyMobiles in collaboration with OnLeaks, reveal that the Pixel 11 and Pixel 11 Pro phones will each be offered in four color options. The standard Pixel 11 is said to arrive in Fuchsia, Light Sterling, Moss, and Midnight Haze. Fuchsia appears to be a vibrant pink finish, making it the boldest color option in...

title: Android 17's new iOS migration tool now available on Samsung's latest foldables
description: Google announced an upgraded Android Switch iOS migration tool for Android 17 during the Android Show I/O Edition 2026. The company has now offered new details about the feature and its availability. The enhanced Android Switch tool is now available on the newly launched Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8, Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, and Galaxy Z Flip8. It is also rolling out to the Pixel 8, Pixel 9, and Pixel 10 series running Android 17. However, Pixel 6 and Pixel 7 series users will not receive the upgraded iOS migration tool. Google added that support for more Android devices will be introduced...

title: Google Pixel Watch 5's key specs revealed via Google Play Console listing
description: The Google Pixel Watch 5 is expected to go official on August 12 alongside the Pixel 11 series smartphones. Recently leaked renders revealed the Pixel Watch 5 will look identical to the Pixel Watch 4. Now a Google Play Console listing reveals the Pixel Watch 5's CPU and GPU will also be the same as its predecessor. The Google Pixel Watch 5, codenamed "godric," is listed on the Google Play Console with Qualcomm's SW5100 SoC, the same base chip used by Pixel Watch 2, 3, and 4. The Pixel Watch 5 also has four Cortex-A53 cores clocked at 1.7GHz and an Adreno 702 GPU, like last year's Pixel...

title: Indian Redmi Note 17 runs Geekbench, reveals its chipset and RAM amount
description: Redmi unveiled the Note 17 and Note 17 Pro in China earlier this month, and now it looks like the Note 17 headed to India has just run Geekbench. As usual, this means there's a listing for it in the Geekbench online database, and that it reveals the chipset at the helm. It's the Snapdragon 4 Gen 4 SoC, same as in the Chinese Note 17. This is aided by 8GB of RAM in the specific unit that ran the benchmark. The phone runs Android 16. As you can see in the screenshot below, it managed a 950 single-core score and a 2,324 multi-core score in Geekbench 6.7.1. According to a recent rumor,...

title: Oppo Find X10 family is being tested against the iPhone 17 Pro, live shots prove
description: According to new information from a tipster on X, Oppo is currently testing the upcoming Find X10, Find X10 Pro, and Find X10 Pro Max alongside the iPhone 17 Pro, presumably to compare their camera prowess against Apple’s latest flagship. The images below seem to show all three devices in a rig with the iPhone 17 Pro, and even though the Oppos are inside a case that's meant to obscure their designs, we can make out the fact that they all appear to have an iPhone-like two-part rear panel design. Or, to put it another way, an iPhone-like gigantic rectangular camera island that stretches...

title: Sony launches FX5 cinema camera with open gate recording
description: Sony has launched the newest member of its compact cinema camera series in the form of the FX5. In another timeline, the FX5 may have just been an update to the Sony FX3 that launched in 2021, but in the current market it will be launching at a higher price, while the FX3 continues to be on sale. The FX5 launches with enough new toys to make the current and potential FX3 buyers jump ship. It has a new 16.6MP full-frame fully-stacked Exmor RS CMOS sensor along with a BIONZ XR2 processor with built-in AI processing. You can record up to 5K 60fps and 4K 120fps video right now, and 5K...

title: Apple's Mac roadmap for the next two years leaks
description: According to a new report from Bloomberg, Apple is planning to release a new entry-level MacBook Pro powered by the M6 chip before the end of this year. The same time frame allegedly also applies to some new iMacs. The MacBook Ultra with OLED touchscreen, expected to be offered in 14" and 16" sizes, is said to be "on track for release between the end of this year and early next year". This will be powered by the M5 Pro and M5 Max chips. The Ultra is reportedly being tested with macOS 27.1, which is expected to become available at the end of October, so the hardware is definitely not...

title: Here is the pricing breakdown for the Galaxy Z Fold8 series and the Z Flip8
description: We’re still at the Galaxy Unpacked event that brought the Galaxy Z Flip8, the Galaxy Z Fold8 and the Z Fold8 Ultra. If you want a closer look, we have a hands-on comparison of the Z Fold8 duo. And now we have to deliver some bad news – the foldables got more expensive. Looking at the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip8, it’s $100/€100/£100 more than the Z Flip7. And this time around we can’t point to an FE model as the cheaper option. The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra is similarly $100/€100/£100 more expensive than its predecessor – that would be the Z Fold7, since Samsung added an “Ultra” tag to...

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra2 and Watch Series 9 hands-on review
description: Introduction In keeping with its usual historic timeframe, Samsung just unveiled its latest crop of foldable smartphones. These include the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, which, confusingly-enough is directly succeeding the Z Fold7, the Z Fold8 - which is now the name for Samsung's entirely new foldable form factor and last and probably least - the Z Flip8. The Korean giant also refreshed its wearable lineup. Naming and general model designations are a lot simpler here. You have a brand new Galaxy Watch Ultra2 only available in a single 47mm size, and then you have the new Galaxy Watch9, which...

title: Samsung unveils AI smart glasses powered by Android XR
description: The Galaxy XR headset is great for gaming and professional uses, but it is not something that you will want to wear while you are out and about. Instead, you might put on the new smart glasses that Samsung unveiled at its Galaxy Unpacked event. The digital side of things is a collaboration between Samsung, Google and Qualcomm. The glasses run Android XR on a Snapdragon AR1 Gen 1 chipset. Unlike the XR2+ Gen 2 chip inside the Galaxy XR, the AR1 chip has been specifically designed for thin and light smart glasses. Samsung smart glasses designed by Gentle Monster The glasses...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 vs Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra: hands-on comparison
description: Samsung was the first major smartphone maker to commit to foldables with the Galaxy Fold in 2019. Since then, it has introduced the Z Flip and now a third form factor enters the race. No, Samsung isn’t the first maker to build a wide foldable, but it is certainly the company with the widest reach that can popularize the form factor. We got some hands-on time with both the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8, which is what the wide foldable is called, and the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra – this is the new name for the large book-style foldable and it is the successor to the Galaxy Z Fold7 (we know, we know, the...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8, Z Fold8 Ultra, Z Flip8 hands-on review
The Galaxy Watch Ultra2 is slimmer and has a huge battery, Watch9 arrives in 40mm and 44mm sizes
description: Two years after the introduction of the original Watch Ultra, Samsung is bringing out the Galaxy Watch Ultra2. It’s also upgrading the vanilla Watch, but this year we’re not getting a new Classic. The new models use the Snapdragon Wear Elite chipset over Samsung’s own Exynos W-series chips. They feature bright displays and bigger batteries. The Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra2 boasts the largest battery yet and the brightest display ever on a Galaxy watch. We’ll get to the display in a moment, but we want to call out the 800mAh battery first – this is a huge upgrade over the 590mAh battery of...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip8 debuts with an upgraded chipset
description: After months of leaks and rumors, Samsung finally unveiled its latest Galaxy Z foldables, including the new Galaxy Z Flip8, at an Unpacked event in London. Samsung’s newest clamshell foldable brings a new chipset and improved software with OneUI 9. The Galaxy Z Flip8 looks identical to its predecessor in terms of design, but is now powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy in the US and an Exynos 2600 chipset in other regions. The display specs also remain the same as the Galaxy Z Flip7. The new clamshell foldable sports a 6.9-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X display on the inside...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 goes wide, Fold8 Ultra aims for the foldable crown
description: Samsung describes the new Galaxy Z series as “its most complete lineup yet,” suggesting that something was missing until now. And that something is the wide foldable that will be known simply as the Galaxy Z Fold8. It’s important to note that this is not the successor to the Z Fold7 – that would be the Z Fold8 Ultra instead. Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 splits the difference between the Flip and the original Fold form factors. The Z Flip8 has a 4.1” cover display, the Z Fold8 a 5.5” one and the Z Fold8 Ultra is the biggest at 6.5”. When closed, the Z Fold8 is...

title: HMD unveils Nokia 123 Shield as its 'most affordable water and dust-resistant feature phone'
description: HMD has unveiled a new 2G feature phone called Nokia 123 Shield, which the brand touts as its "most affordable water and dust-resistant feature phone" that's also optimized for "reliable call quality." HMD says the Nokia 123 Shield can withstand rain, splashes, sand, and dust, and while it isn't advertising any IP rating for the phone, a footnote on the official website says the Nokia 123 Shield "meets the requirements of IP65 rating under IEC standard 60529." Furthermore, HMD states that the Nokia 123 Shield is built from the ground up for outdoor workers. It has a slip-resistant...

title: Garmin unveils CIRQA Smart Band, its first screen-free fitness tracker
description: Garmin has announced its first screen-free fitness tracker called the CIRQA Smart Band. Unlike similar alternatives in the market, Garmin’s offering does not require a subscription to access advanced fitness and wellness features. The Garmin CIRQA Smart Band is claimed to deliver up to 10 days of battery life from a single charge even with 24/7 health monitoring. It features a fiber-reinforced polymer case with a fabric strap and can be worn around the wrist or the arm. The module weighs 14.8 grams and gets a 5ATM rating. As for health and fitness tracking, the smart band...
